titlestmt.titleproper: Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century."23-2-2720

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

ead.archdesc:

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century."
New York State College of Human Ecology
23-2-2720
1.3 cubic feet. 1.3 cubic feet.
1992 Undated Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University. Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University. Collection material in English 2248672
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Karyn Hartz
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2017
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, audiocassettes, publicity, lists, and other administrative records of the conference at Cornell University.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Panelists/Attendees Box 1 Folders 1-61
Series II. Arrangements/Publicity Box 1 Folders 62-76Box 2 Folders 1-17
Series III. Cassette Tapes Box 2
Cite As:
Conference records: "Rethinking Women and Home Economics in the Twentieth Century," #23-2-2720. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Women -- Social conditions. Home economics.
2248672 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Panelists/AttendeesAllen, Ruth - commentary and correspondence11Apple, Rina - University of Wisconsin - "Every Girl a Mother Home Economics and the Ideology of Motherhood, 1890-1950"12Babbitt, Kathleen - SUNY Binghamton - "Alphabet Soup and Depression-Era Subsistence: NYS Extension and the New Deal Agencies of the 1930s"13Bates, Mercedes correspondence14Belesco, Warren - University of Maryland, Baltimore County - Cassette Tapes1st session of conference21991-10-05 Alumni Auditorium 1:20pm1991-10-0521991-10-05 Alumni Auditorium 3:00pm1991-10-0521991-10-05 Alumni Auditorium 4:45pm1991-10-052Intro by Eileen DeVault, Lynn Nyhart: "Home Economists in the Hospital, 1900-1930," Carolyn Goldstein: "Part of the Package: Home Economists in Industry During the Interwar Period," Lois Post: commentary - what is was like to be Betty Crocker2Intro by SG Khohlstedt, Sarah Stage on Ellen Swallow Richards and the Lake Placid conferences, Nancy Tomes "Spreading the Germ Theory"2Intro by Margaret Washington, Rose Marie Weber: "Starved for Something to Read: Literacy in the Cornell Reading Course for Farmers' Wives," Cramen Harris: "Grace Under Pressure: The African-American Home Economics Agent in South Carolina, 1919-1965," comment by Florence Low on papers and on black home economics agents in Texas in the 1930s, during WWII and during integration2Intro by Ann Lane, Rima Apple: "Every Girl a Mother: Home Economics and the Ideology of Motherhood, 1890-1950," Julia Grant: "Modernizing Mothers: Home Economics and the Parent Eduction Movement," Comments: Henry Ricciuti and Lane2Hersey2 ======= Series I.
